actionLibraryList.title: Seated 90/90 Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Alternatively rotate your hips, keep your upper body upright, and maintain a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sit on the floor with your legs split. Bend one knee and bring it out to the side, creating a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Alternatively roatate your hip, keep your upper body upright, always keep a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Complete the full range of motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718178177.jpg","context":"1.Rotate your hips alternately.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine position.\n3.Keep your hips and knees at a 90° angle throughout."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Ensure a full range of motion.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated 90/90 Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Alternatively rotate your hips, keep your upper body upright, and maintain a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sit on the floor with your legs split. Bend one knee and bring it out to the side, creating a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Alternatively roatate your hip, keep your upper body upright, always keep a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Complete the full range of motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718178177.jpg","context":"1.Rotate your hips alternately.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine position.\n3.Keep your hips and knees at a 90° angle throughout."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Ensure a full range of motion.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grip to ground level.
2.Grab the handles with both hands and bring them to an upright position.
3.Lift your chest, keep your back straight, and engage your core for stability.
4.Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to initiate the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain balance throughout the squat, returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with both handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't arch back when you return to the straight. Don't thrust your hips forward at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Big breath at the start of the movement, engaging your glutes, slowly exhale during the ascent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate standing movement by squeezing your glutes and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your arms naturally extended.\n2.Hips higher than the knees.\n3.Align your knees with your toes.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233975187.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep the knees straight, but do not lock them.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750234126578.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your back as you return to the upright position. Do not over-extend your lower back at the peak contraction.

actionLibraryList.title: Upper Body Rowing
actionLibraryList.context: Sit on the bench, grip the handle, and retract your shoulder blades. Pull the handle to your waist by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body. Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return. Focus on your lats and biceps, keeping your torso stable and avoiding any shoulder shrugging.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750672071926.jpg","context":"1.Start with your arms extended."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750672078477.jpg","context":"1.Bend your elbows and pull them back, keeping them close to your body."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale to finish, inhale to return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel the activation in your lats, scapula, and biceps.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid shrugging, compensating, or excessive movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Cable Side Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core.
2.Attach the band to the working leg's ankle, with the leg pushing against the resistance.
3.Step the working leg out to the side and bend the knee into a lateral lunge.
4.Keep the other leg straight and lean your upper body slightly toward the working leg.
5.Drive through the working leg and glutes to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750407767763.jpg","context":"1.Working leg pushes against the band."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750407772432.jpg","context":"1.Step the working leg out to the side and bend the knee into a lateral lunge.\n2.Keep the other leg straight with the knee facing forward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat down, exhale as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Strong activation in the glutes and legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the knee from collapsing inward.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Dual-Handle Z Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit on the footboard with legs extended, core engaged.
2.Grip the handles at shoulder height with wrists neutral.
3.Press the handles upward until arms are nearly straight without locking out.
4.Pause briefly at the top, then lower back to shoulder level under control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748144879633.jpg","context":"1.Legs extended.\n2.Keep your back straight."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748144892541.jpg","context":"1.Press upward until the arms are nearly straight without locking out."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you press, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid shrugging and leaning back throughout the movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Flutter Kicks
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back, engage your core, and lift your legs a few centimeters off the floor, keeping them straight.
2.If necessary, use your arms for support, then begin alternating leg kicks.
3.Ensure that your back maintains a neutral position throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lying on floor, engage core and hold legs straight and a few cm off the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Using arms to support if need, start alternating kicks with legs.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Be sure to not let back lose its neutral position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep a smooth breath and don't hold your breath.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Alternating kicks with legs by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Place your hands under your hips and keep your core engaged throughout.\n2.Hang your legs in the air and alternate lifting each leg.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056195741.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Maintain a steady breath and avoid holding your breath.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Perform alternating leg kicks by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Make sure to keep the back in a neutral position.
